title: Dry Broiled Kenyan Lamb Kebabs
description: Savory and snappy lamb kebabs featuring lamb stock, broiled to perfection, and served as a paleo-friendly dish. This energizing Kenyan dish is perfect for a dinner meal.
instructions: Preheat the broiler to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together lamb stock, olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each cube.
Place the skewers on a broiler pan lined with aluminum foil and broil for 10-12 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Remove the skewers from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the lamb kebabs hot, garnished with fresh cilantro or parsley.
Repeat the broiling process for any remaining skewers.
Let the lamb kebabs cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
